description_long: This is a pair of vintage metal scissors with a visibly aged and weathered surface, showing signs of rust and patina. The handles are asymmetrical, with one loop larger than the other, and both are oval-shaped, designed for finger and thumb placement. The blades are thick, robust, and slightly stained, with a straight edge and a pointed tip for precise cutting. The joint connecting the blades is a fixed rivet, visible at the intersection, indicating a non-adjustable, sturdy construction. The overall finish is matte with a rough texture, suggesting frequent use and exposure to the elements. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the surface.
description: A weathered, vintage pair of metal scissors with asymmetrical oval handles and pointed, stained blades joined by a visible rivet.
description_view_0: Front view showing both oval handles and the full length of the blades, highlighting the asymmetrical handle design and visible rivet.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the blades and handles, with the rivet joint visible at the midpoint.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the handles and blades, with rust and patina more apparent on the blade surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the handles and blades.
attribute_handle_type: Asymmetrical oval handles, uncoated, not padded, designed for right-handed use, no ergonomic or special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Fixed rivet joint, visible, non-adjustable, standard design.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two oval loops, one larger than the other, smooth metal, no inserts or texturing, accommodates thumb and two fingers.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed blade tips, designed for precision cutting.
